etymology.description: 品(品) (pǐn, "three mouths") + 木(木) (mù, "tree") = chirping of birds in a tree -> original form of 噪(噪) (zào, "noise")
etymology.interpretation: This character combines the imagery of three mouths 品(品) (pǐn, "three mouths") representing multiple sounds and 木(木) (mù, "tree"), depicting the noise created by birds chirping in a tree. It was later used as the original form for 噪(噪).
